commit_summaries.message:
[BugFix] anthropic/serving_messages: fix tool call arguments streaming

The `/v1/messages` endpoint returns invalid tool call arguments json
when using mistral models, such as `mistralai/Ministral-3-14B-Instruct-2512.`.

json is invalid because the first token (e.g. the opening `{\"` is
always missinfg)

The reason is that mistral models generate the first delta for tool
calls along with the arguments delta, like this

```json
"delta": {
    "tool_calls": [
        {"id": "O4kbyHTo9", "type": "function", "index": 0, "function": { "name": "execute_bash_code", "arguments": "{\"" } }}
    ]
}
```

while the current implementation of `message_stream_converter` generates a `AnthropicStreamEvent(type="content_block_start", ...)` event
and streams it back to the client whenever a tool call block with a non-empty id is processed, but the tool call arguments are ignored
if present (incorrectly assuming that the first delta will only contain the tool call id and function name.

The solution is to create and stream an `AnthropicStreamEvent(type='content_block_delta', ...)` with the partial json function arguments right after
the `content_block_start` event is created and streamed.

Signed-off-by: Daniele Trifirò <dtrifiro@redhat.com>
